title: Air-fryer garlic butter prawns
subtitle: King prawns in garlic butter in under 8 minutes
excerpt: Raw king prawns tossed in melted butter, crushed garlic, and parsley, cooked in the air fryer at 200°C for 6 to 8 minutes. Prawns cook fast and the air fryer handles them without any attention. Use raw prawns only: pre-cooked prawns become rubbery when reheated at high temperature.
sourceNotes: Garlic butter prawns (gambas al ajillo in Spain, gamberi all'aglio in Italy) is one of the most widely cooked shellfish dishes in British home cooking. The air fryer version became popular because it produces the same result as a hot pan without any oil spatter and allows the prawns to cook in a single layer without crowding.

content.text: Use raw prawns, not pre-cooked ones. Pre-cooked prawns are already at the end of their cooking journey; putting them back in the air fryer at 200°C takes them past the point of doneness in under 3 minutes, resulting in a rubbery, tough texture. Raw prawns go from translucent and soft to pink and firm in exactly the right time.

Pat the {{prawns-raw}} dry with kitchen paper. Toss with the melted {{unsalted-butter}}, {{garlic}}, {{chilli-flakes}} if using, and {{sea-salt-fine}}. Preheat the air fryer to 200°C for 2 minutes. Spread the prawns in a single layer in the basket, not piled on top of each other. Cook at 200°C for 6 minutes for smaller king prawns or 7 to 8 minutes for large ones. The prawns are done when fully pink and opaque throughout with a slight curl. Do not cook past this point. Remove immediately and scatter with {{parsley-flat}}. Serve with a squeeze of {{lemon}}.

items.fix: Prawns overcook in seconds at high heat. Check at 6 minutes and remove as soon as they are uniformly pink and curled. Pre-cooked prawns should only be warmed through in the air fryer at 160°C for 3 minutes at most
items.cause: Overcooked, or pre-cooked prawns used instead of raw

items.fix: Make sure the prawns are coated in the garlic butter mixture rather than having chunks of garlic sitting loose. If your air fryer runs very hot, reduce to 180°C and add 2 minutes
items.cause: Crushed garlic sitting directly on the basket at 200°C
items.symptom: Garlic burning before prawns are cooked

items.fix: Pat the prawns dry first. Spread in a single layer with gaps between them. Cook in two batches if needed. Overlapping prawns steam each other and will not develop any colour
items.cause: Basket overcrowded, or prawns not dried before cooking

content.text: Air-fryer garlic butter prawns are a fast starter or a quick weeknight main. Serve as a starter with crusty bread to mop up the butter, over linguine with a splash of white wine added to the cooking juices, or as a sharing plate with rice and a simple green salad. The whole dish is ready in under 15 minutes from start to plate.
